verses.latin_vulgate: Respondens autem Eliphaz Themanites, dixit :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַ֭יַּעַן אֱלִיפַ֥ז הַֽתֵּימָנִ֗י וַיֹּאמַֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Eliphaz, the Themanite, answered, and said:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Eliphas Temanilainen vastasi ja sanoi,
verses.king_james: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said,

verses.latin_vulgate: Si cœperimus loqui tibi, forsitan moleste accipies ;  sed conceptum sermonem tenere quis poterit ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲנִסָּ֬ה דָבָ֣ר אֵלֶ֣יךָ תִּלְאֶ֑ה וַעְצֹ֥ר בְּ֝מִלִּ֗ין מִ֣י יוּכָֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: If we begin to speak to thee, perhaps thou wilt take it ill; but who can withhold the words he hath conceived?
verses.finnish_biblia: Jos joku sinun kanssas rupeis puhumaan: mitämaks et sinä sitä kärsi; mutta kuka taitaa vaiti olla?
verses.king_james: If we assay to commune with thee, wilt thou be grieved? but who can withhold himself from speaking?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce docuisti multos,  et manus lassas roborasti ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִ֭נֵּה יִסַּ֣רְתָּ רַבִּ֑ים וְיָדַ֖יִם רָפ֣וֹת תְּחַזֵּֽק׃
verses.douay_rheims: Behold thou hast taught many, and thou hast strengthened the weary hands:
verses.finnish_biblia: Katso, sinä olet monta neuvonut, ja vahvistanut väsyneitä käsiä.
verses.king_james: Behold, thou hast instructed many, and thou hast strengthened the weak hands.

verses.latin_vulgate: vacillantes confirmaverunt sermones tui,  et genua trementia confortasti.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כּ֭וֹשֵׁל יְקִימ֣וּן מִלֶּ֑יךָ וּבִרְכַּ֖יִם כֹּרְע֣וֹת תְּאַמֵּֽץ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thy words have confirmed them that were staggering, and thou hast strengthened the trembling knees:
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinun puhees on ojentanut langenneita, ja vapisevaisia polvia vahvistanut.
verses.king_james: Thy words have upholden him that was falling, and thou hast strengthened the feeble knees.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc autem venit super te plaga, et defecisti ;  tetigit te, et conturbatus es.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י עַתָּ֨ה ׀ תָּב֣וֹא אֵלֶ֣יךָ וַתֵּ֑לֶא תִּגַּ֥ע עָ֝דֶ֗יךָ וַתִּבָּהֵֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: But now the scourge is come upon thee, and thou faintest: It hath touched thee, and thou art troubled.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta että se nyt tulee sinun päälles, niin sinä väsyit, ja että se lankesi sinun päälles, niin sinä peljästyit.
verses.king_james: But now it is come upon thee, and thou faintest; it toucheth thee, and thou art troubled.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ubi est timor tuus, fortitudo tua,  patientia tua, et perfectio viarum tuarum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲלֹ֣א יִ֭רְאָתְךָ כִּסְלָתֶ֑ךָ תִּ֝קְוָתְךָ֗ וְתֹ֣ם דְּרָכֶֽיךָ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Where is thy fear, thy fortitude, thy patience, and the perfection of thy ways?
verses.finnish_biblia: Tämäkö on sinun (Jumalan) pelkos, sinun uskallukses, sinun toivos ja sinun vakuutes?
verses.king_james: Is not this thy fear, thy confidence, thy hope, and the uprightness of thy ways?

verses.latin_vulgate: Recordare, obsecro te, quis umquam innocens periit ?  aut quando recti deleti sunt ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: זְכׇר־נָ֗א מִ֤י ה֣וּא נָקִ֣י אָבָ֑ד וְ֝אֵיפֹ֗ה יְשָׁרִ֥ים נִכְחָֽדוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Remember, I pray thee, who ever perished being innocent? or when were the just destroyed?
verses.finnish_biblia: Muistele nyt, kussa joku viatoin on hukkunut? eli kussa hurskaat ovat joskus hävinneet?
verses.king_james: Remember, I pray thee, who ever perished, being innocent? or where were the righteous cut off?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quin potius vidi eos qui operantur iniquitatem,  et seminant dolores, et metunt eos,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כַּאֲשֶׁ֣ר רָ֭אִיתִי חֹ֣רְשֵׁי אָ֑וֶן וְזֹרְעֵ֖י עָמָ֣ל יִקְצְרֻֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: On the contrary, I have seen those who work iniquity, and sow sorrows, and reap them,
verses.finnish_biblia: Niinkuin minä kyllä nähnyt olen, ne jotka kyntävät vääryyden, ja onnettomuuden kylvävät, sitä he myös niittävät.
verses.king_james: Even as I have seen, they that plow iniquity, and sow wickedness, reap the same.

verses.latin_vulgate: flante Deo perisse,  et spiritu iræ ejus esse consumptos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִנִּשְׁמַ֣ת אֱל֣וֹהַּ יֹאבֵ֑דוּ וּמֵר֖וּחַ אַפּ֣וֹ יִכְלֽוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Perishing by the blast of God, and consumed by the spirit of his wrath.
verses.finnish_biblia: He ovat Jumalan puhalluksen kautta kadonneet, ja hänen vihansa hengeltä hukatut.
verses.king_james: By the blast of God they perish, and by the breath of his nostrils are they consumed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Rugitus leonis, et vox leænæ,  et dentes catulorum leonum contriti sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שַׁאֲגַ֣ת אַ֭רְיֵה וְק֣וֹל שָׁ֑חַל וְשִׁנֵּ֖י כְפִירִ֣ים נִתָּֽעוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The roaring of the lion, and the voice of the lioness, and the teeth of the whelps of lions, are broken:
verses.finnish_biblia: Jalopeuran kiljumus, ja julma jalopeuran ääni, ja nuorten jalopeurain hampaat ovat lovistetut.
verses.king_james: The roaring of the lion, and the voice of the fierce lion, and the teeth of the young lions, are broken.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tigris periit, eo quod non haberet prædam,  et catuli leonis dissipati sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לַ֭יִשׁ אֹבֵ֣ד מִבְּלִי־טָ֑רֶף וּבְנֵ֥י לָ֝בִ֗יא יִתְפָּרָֽדוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The tiger hath perished for want of prey, and the young lions are scattered abroad.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vanha jalopeura hukkuu, ettei hänellä ole saalista; ja jalopeuran pojat hajoitetaan.
verses.king_james: The old lion perisheth for lack of prey, and the stout lion’s whelps are scattered abroad.

verses.latin_vulgate: Porro ad me dictum est verbum absconditum,  et quasi furtive suscepit auris mea venas susurri ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְ֭אֵלַי דָּבָ֣ר יְגֻנָּ֑ב וַתִּקַּ֥ח אׇ֝זְנִ֗י שֵׁ֣מֶץ מֶֽנְהֽוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Now there was a word spoken to me in private, and my ears by stealth, as it were, received the veins of its whisper.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja minun tyköni on tullut salainen sana; ja minun korvani on saanut vähäisen siitä.
verses.king_james: Now a thing was secretly brought to me, and mine ear received a little thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: In horrore visionis nocturnæ,  quando solet sopor occupare homines,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בִּ֭שְׂעִפִּים מֵחֶזְיֹנ֣וֹת לָ֑יְלָה בִּנְפֹ֥ל תַּ֝רְדֵּמָ֗ה עַל־אֲנָשִֽׁים׃
verses.douay_rheims: In the horror of a vision by night, when deep sleep is wont to hold men,
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuin minä ajattelin yönäkyjä, kuin uni lankee ihmisten päälle,
verses.king_james: In thoughts from the visions of the night, when deep sleep falleth on men,

verses.latin_vulgate: pavor tenuit me, et tremor,  et omnia ossa mea perterrita sunt ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: פַּ֣חַד קְ֭רָאַנִי וּרְעָדָ֑ה וְרֹ֖ב עַצְמוֹתַ֣י הִפְחִֽיד׃
verses.douay_rheims: Fear seized upon me, and trembling, and all my bones were affrighted:
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin pelko ja vavistus tuli minun päälleni, ja kaikki minun luuni peljätettiin;
verses.king_james: Fear came upon me, and trembling, which made all my bones to shake.

verses.latin_vulgate: et cum spiritus, me præsente, transiret,  inhorruerunt pili carnis meæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְ֭רוּחַ עַל־פָּנַ֣י יַחֲלֹ֑ף תְּ֝סַמֵּ֗ר שַׂעֲרַ֥ת בְּשָׂרִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And when a spirit passed before me, the hair of my flesh stood up.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja henki meni minun sivuitseni; kaikki minun karvani nousivat ruumiissani.
verses.king_james: Then a spirit passed before my face; the hair of my flesh stood up:

verses.latin_vulgate: Stetit quidam, cujus non agnoscebam vultum,  imago coram oculis meis,  et vocem quasi auræ lenis audivi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַעֲמֹ֤ד ׀ וְֽלֹא־אַכִּ֬יר מַרְאֵ֗הוּ תְּ֭מוּנָה לְנֶ֣גֶד עֵינָ֑י דְּמָמָ֖ה וָק֣וֹל אֶשְׁמָֽע׃
verses.douay_rheims: There stood one whose countenance I knew not, an image before my eyes, and I heard the voice, as it were, of a gentle wind.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin seisoi kuva minun silmäini edessä, jonka kasvoja en minä tuntenut; ja minä kuulin hiljaisen hyminän äänen:
verses.king_james: It stood still, but I could not discern the form thereof: an image was before mine eyes, there was silence, and I heard a voice, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Numquid homo, Dei comparatione, justificabitur ?  aut factore suo purior erit vir ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַ֭אֱנוֹשׁ מֵאֱל֣וֹהַּ יִצְדָּ֑ק אִ֥ם מֵ֝עֹשֵׂ֗הוּ יִטְהַר־גָּֽבֶר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Shall man be justified in comparison of God, or shall a man be more pure than his maker?
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuinka on ihminen hurskaampi kuin JumaIa? eli joku mies puhtaampi kuin se, joka hänen tehnyt on?
verses.king_james: Shall mortal man be more just than God? shall a man be more pure than his maker?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce qui serviunt ei, non sunt stabiles,  et in angelis suis reperit pravitatem ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵ֣ן בַּ֭עֲבָדָיו לֹ֣א יַאֲמִ֑ין וּ֝בְמַלְאָכָ֗יו יָשִׂ֥ים תׇּהֳלָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Behold, they that serve him are not steadfast, and in his angels he found wickedness:
verses.finnish_biblia: Katso, palveliainsa seassa ei löydä hän uskollisuutta ja enkeleissänsä löytää hän tyhmyyden:
verses.king_james: Behold, he put no trust in his servants; and his angels he charged with folly:

verses.latin_vulgate: quanto magis hi qui habitant domos luteas,  qui terrenum habent fundamentum,  consumentur velut a tinea ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַ֤ף ׀ שֹׁכְנֵ֬י בָֽתֵּי־חֹ֗מֶר אֲשֶׁר־בֶּעָפָ֥ר יְסוֹדָ֑ם יְ֝דַכְּא֗וּם לִפְנֵי־עָֽשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: How much more shall they that dwell in houses of clay, who have an earthly foundation, be consumed as with the moth?
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuinka enemmin niissä jotka asuvat savihuoneissa, niissä jotka ovat perustetut maan päälle, jotka toukkain tavalla murentuvat?
verses.king_james: How much less in them that dwell in houses of clay, whose foundation is in the dust, which are crushed before the moth?

verses.latin_vulgate: De mane usque ad vesperam succidentur ;  et quia nullus intelligit, in æternum peribunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִבֹּ֣קֶר לָעֶ֣רֶב יֻכַּ֑תּוּ מִבְּלִ֥י מֵ֝שִׂ֗ים לָנֶ֥צַח יֹאבֵֽדוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: From morning till evening they shall be cut down: and because no one understandeth, they shall perish for ever.
verses.finnish_biblia: Se pysyy aamusta ehtoosen asti, niin he muserretaan; ja ennenkuin he sen havaitsevat, he ijankaikkisesti hukkuvat.
verses.king_james: They are destroyed from morning to evening: they perish for ever without any regarding it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui autem reliqui fuerint, auferentur ex eis ;  morientur, et non in sapientia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֲלֹא־נִסַּ֣ע יִתְרָ֣ם בָּ֑ם יָ֝מ֗וּתוּ וְלֹ֣א בְחׇכְמָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they that shall be left, shall be taken away from them: they shall die, and not in wisdom.
verses.finnish_biblia: Eikö heidän jälkeenjääneensä pois oteta? he kuolevat, vaan ei viisaudessa.
verses.king_james: Doth not their excellency which is in them go away? they die, even without wisdom.
